transcript:
Agent: Good morning, HealthFirst Patient Services. This is Rachel. How can I assist you?
Customer: Hi Rachel. I need a refill for my blood pressure medication but my prescription expired last week. My doctor's office isn't picking up.
Agent: I understand. Let me help you navigate this. Can I get your patient ID or date of birth?
Customer: Date of birth is March 15, 1968. Patient ID is HF-334892.
Agent: Thank you. I can see your account. The medication is Lisinopril 10mg, is that correct?
Customer: Yes, that's the one.
Agent: I can see the prescription expired on February 18th. Your prescribing physician is Dr. Martinez. I can send an urgent refill request directly to their office on your behalf.
Customer: I'd appreciate that. I've been without it for three days.
Agent: That's a concern with blood pressure medication. I'm sending the urgent request now and marking it as a gap-in-care situation. Dr. Martinez's office is required to respond within 24 hours for urgent requests.
Customer: What if they don't respond? I can't go much longer without it.
Agent: In that case, you have two options. You can visit one of our urgent care partners and the on-duty physician can write a bridge prescription for a 30-day supply. Or if you have a preferred pharmacy, some pharmacists can provide an emergency short-term supply under state regulations.
Customer: The urgent care option sounds more reliable. Is there one near zip code 94102?
Agent: Yes, HealthFirst Urgent Care on Market Street is 0.8 miles from you. I can pre-register you to reduce wait time. Hours are 8 AM to 8 PM.
Customer: Please do pre-register me. I'll head there this afternoon.
Agent: Done. Your pre-registration confirmation is HFU-20240226-334892. I've also sent the urgent refill request to Dr. Martinez so you may still get a response today.
Customer: This is such a relief. Thank you for handling all of this, Rachel.
Agent: Of course. Managing a gap in critical medication is exactly what we're here for. Take care and feel better!
